title: Mullin's March and the Curse of the 16th
title_candidates: Mullin's March and the Curse of the 16th
Wire to Wire, Wobble to Wobble
theme: David Mullin leads from the first afternoon at Deal to the last green at Prince's, never headed in either competition — but the lead shrinks from nine after R2 to two at the death as Alex Baker mounts a charge that is repeatedly torpedoed by his own catastrophes, most notably twin 10s at the same 16th hole.
opening_hook: Open on Prince's, R4, the 16th: Alex Baker — Mullin's lone pursuer — standing on the tee within touching distance, and walking off with a 10. Then rewind to Deal four days earlier.
foreshadow: A first-afternoon takeover at Deal that nobody quite registers as the takeover for the whole week
The 16th at Royal Cinque Ports — where Alex Baker's first 10 is filed under 'links golf, these things happen'
A nine-point cushion after Littlestone that looks like a procession but is actually the high-water mark
Mullin's own scorecard sprouting big numbers at Prince's, hinting the lead is more fragile than it looks

competitions.name: Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.how: Wire-to-wire on 154 points, by 2 from Alex Baker. Mullin drew level on R1 H11 and took outright control on R1 H16 — and was never headed again across the next 56 holes. Rounds of 40 and 41 at Deal and Littlestone built a nine-point cushion; 36 at Prince's kept it at eight. The wobble came in R4, where quad-bogey blowups (notably the 10th) bled the lead down to two — but Alex Baker's own R4 16th (a 10 of his own) snuffed out the only credible challenge.

competitions.name: Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: A second wire-to-wire, won by 7 at +68 over Jon Baker. Alex Baker briefly drew level at R1 H6 before Mullin pulled even again at the 9th and never looked back; gross rounds of 15 and 14 at Deal and Littlestone effectively settled it, the 12-shot R2 lead never seriously threatened despite scruffier 20 and 19 at Prince's. A personal-best gross total (+76) from Gregg Williams was the sub-plot.

competitions.name: Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: Owned outright from R1 H4 and never relinquished — last in all four rounds on the Stableford count, finishing on 125. The cruel twist: Baker actually produced the best closing round of the four (39 points at Prince's R4), including a 14-point flurry across holes 12–15, but it came far too late, and an 8 at the 18th was a fitting kiss-off. He was, however, runner-up in the Jacket — a perfectly normal split of handicapped and raw scoring, not a riddle.

headline_candidates: Deal Me In: Mullin Takes Charge at the Coast
Alex Baker and the 16th, Part One
Wire One: Mullin in Front by Tea-Time
rounds.chosen_headline: Deal Me In: Mullin Takes Charge at the Coast
rounds.angle: At a returning Royal Cinque Ports, the tournament's whole shape is set in an afternoon: Mullin level by the 11th, outright by the 16th, with a 40-point card and a five-shot gross cushion. Alex Baker's 10 at the 16th and quad at the 11th hint at the pattern to come; Jon Baker drops to last as early as the 4th and stays there.

headline_candidates: 41 and Counting: Mullin Pulls Clear at Littlestone
rounds.chosen_headline: 41 and Counting: Mullin Pulls Clear at Littlestone
rounds.angle: The high-water mark. Mullin's 41 stretches the Trophy lead to nine and the Jacket lead to twelve. Alex Baker hands back the chase with a brutal 2–5 stretch (12 shots dropped) and twin quads at the 5th and 8th. Mullin himself shows he can stop the bleeding when he has to.

headline_candidates: Prince's I: Mullin Wobbles, Mullin Wins
rounds.chosen_headline: Prince's I: Mullin Wobbles, Mullin Wins
rounds.angle: First crack of light for the chasers — Mullin makes a 9 at the 8th — but he answers with a closing four-hole 13-point flourish and a sharply better back nine. Alex Baker quads the 10th, Jon Baker the 13th, Williams's tidy run ends at the 12th. Lead trimmed to eight, but only just.

The day the procession nearly turned into a race. Mullin makes an 8 at the 10th, the lead is genuinely shrinking, and Alex Baker is two pars away from real pressure when he walks off the 16th with a 10 — at the same hole number that had him in R1. Jon Baker, free of the spoon arithmetic, posts the best round of the day (39) with a 14-point burst at 12–15 before an 8 at the last. Mullin home by two and seven.

players.arc: Champion in both books from the first afternoon — wire-to-wire on Stableford and Gross, a 40-41 start that effectively decided it, and enough resilience to absorb his own 9 and 8 down the stretch at Prince's.

Trophy runner-up and the week's tragicomic figure: capable of stretches like 11 points in three holes, but undone by an outsized collection of blowups — quads at Deal's 11th, Littlestone's 5th and 8th, Prince's 10th, and the two 10s at the 16th that bookended his tournament.

Last on Stableford from the 4th hole of R1 to the 72nd, yet runner-up for the Jacket and author of the best round of the week — a 39 at Prince's including a 14-point flurry, spoiled only by a closing 8.
Quiet personal-best week on the gross card (+76), with a tidy R3 hot stretch the highlight before his customary stumble (a triple at the 12th) and an early Deal quad.

venue_notes: TEG's second visit to Kent (after the original Kent tour). Open with Royal Cinque Ports as 'a formidable championship links' returning to the schedule — the 2nd TEG round at Deal. Littlestone framed as the Purves/Braid/MacKenzie pedigree course on the Romney Marshes (2nd visit). Prince's Shore/Dunes did the double duty as Rounds 3 and 4 — 2nd and 3rd TEG rounds at the venue — and the 16th of that routing is, by the end of the week, Alex Baker's personal nemesis.
